audit_items.proof_trace: When calloc succeeds but mutex_trylock fails (returns non-zero, i.e. lock held during hash expansion), the else branch at L313 executes `return NULL` without calling free(iter). The caller receives NULL and has no reference to the allocation, making it permanently unreachable. No other mechanism frees iter on this path.
audit_items.attack_scenario: The LRU crawler thread calls assoc_get_iterator() periodically; if hash table expansion holds maintenance_lock, mutex_trylock fails and ~32 bytes leak per invocation. Repeated crawler cycles during sustained hash expansion lead to unbounded heap growth and eventual memory exhaustion.
